body:
Title: CRA Form T2203 2025 Part 3 Newfoundland and Labrador, Prince Edward Island, and Nova Scotia non-refundable tax credits.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/t2203/t2203-25e.pdf

CRA T2203 2025 Part 3 calculates provincial non-refundable tax credits for a multiple-jurisdiction return. For Newfoundland and Labrador, the form uses a basic personal amount of $11,067 on line 58040, a low credit rate of 8.7% encoded as 0.087, and a Schedule 9 high-rate credit rate of 21.8% encoded as 0.218. The Newfoundland and Labrador age amount worksheet line 58080 uses a maximum of $7,064, an income threshold of $38,712, and a 15% reduction rate encoded as 0.15. The spouse amount worksheet line 58120 uses a base amount of $9,948 and a maximum of $9,043 after the spouse or common-law partner net income reduction. The eligible dependant worksheet line 58160 uses the same $9,948 base and $9,043 maximum. Newfoundland and Labrador Part 3 also includes CPP or QPP contributions, enhanced CPP or QPP contributions, employment insurance premiums, enhanced EI premiums, a $3,000 volunteer firefighters or search and rescue volunteers amount, child care expenses, adoption expenses capped at $14,935 per child, pension income capped at $1,000, caregiver and disability amounts, disability supports, Schedule S11 or S11MJ amounts, student designated amounts, Schedule S2MJ amounts, and medical expenses. The Newfoundland and Labrador medical expense worksheet line ME subtracts the lesser of 3% of net income, encoded as 0.03, and $2,410 from eligible medical expenses.

For Prince Edward Island, the form uses a basic personal amount of $14,650 on line 58040, a low credit rate of 9.5% encoded as 0.095, and a Schedule 9 high-rate credit rate of 19% encoded as 0.19. The Prince Edward Island age amount worksheet line 58080 uses a maximum of $6,510, an income threshold of $36,600, and a 15% reduction rate encoded as 0.15. The spouse amount worksheet line 58120 uses a base amount of $13,687 and a maximum of $12,443 after the spouse or common-law partner net income reduction. The eligible dependant worksheet line 58160 uses the same $13,687 base and $12,443 maximum. The amount for young children line 58230 is the number of qualifying months from line 58229 multiplied by $100. Prince Edward Island Part 3 also includes CPP or QPP contributions, enhanced CPP or QPP contributions, employment insurance premiums, enhanced EI premiums, pension income capped at $1,000, caregiver and disability amounts, the children's wellness tax credit, teacher school supply amount capped at $500, disability supports, Schedule S11 or S11MJ amounts, student designated amounts, Schedule S2MJ amounts, and medical expenses. The Prince Edward Island medical expense worksheet line ME subtracts the lesser of 3% of net income, encoded as 0.03, and $1,678 from eligible medical expenses.

For Nova Scotia, the form uses a basic personal amount of $11,744 on line 58040, a low credit rate of 8.79% encoded as 0.0879, and a Schedule 9 high-rate credit rate of 21% encoded as 0.21. The Nova Scotia age amount worksheet line 58080 uses a maximum of $5,734, an income threshold of $30,828, and a 15% reduction rate encoded as 0.15. The spouse amount worksheet line 58120 uses a base amount of $12,618, a minimum income floor of $874, and a maximum of $11,744. The eligible dependant worksheet line 58160 uses the same $12,618 base, $874 income floor, and $11,744 maximum. The amount for young children line 58230 is the number of qualifying months from line 58229 multiplied by $100. Nova Scotia Part 3 also includes CPP or QPP contributions, enhanced CPP or QPP contributions, employment insurance premiums, enhanced EI premiums, pension income capped at $1,173, caregiver and disability amounts, disability supports, Schedule S11 or S11MJ amounts, student designated amounts, Schedule S2MJ amounts, and medical expenses. The Nova Scotia medical expense worksheet line ME subtracts the lesser of 3% of net income, encoded as 0.03, and $1,637 from eligible medical expenses.

For each province in this Part 3 batch, the encoded subtotal adds the basic personal amount, age amount, spouse amount, eligible dependant amount, selected listed credits, the computed medical expense amount, medical expenses for other dependants, and S2 or S11 transfer lines. The total non-refundable tax credit line D multiplies the subtotal by the province's low credit rate and adds the Schedule 9 high-rate amount computed from line 13 minus line 14 multiplied by the province's Schedule 9 high-rate credit rate. Per-dependant row tables for infirm dependants, caregivers, disability transfers, and medical expenses for other dependants, plus detailed S2 and S11 transfer schedules, are not encoded in this focused module.
